examples.title: Encapsulation Process Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how data is encapsulated at each layer during transmission
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the encapsulation process where each layer adds its header to the data as it moves down from Application to Physical layer
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is data size

examples.title: Decapsulation Process Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how headers are removed at each layer during reception
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Decapsulate(receivedBits)
BEGIN
 // Physical Layer - Convert bits to frame
 frame = CONVERT_FROM_BITS(receivedBits)
 
 // Data Link Layer - Remove frame header/trailer
 packet = REMOVE_FRAME_HEADER(frame)
 VERIFY_MAC(packet)
 
 // Network Layer - Remove packet header
 segment = REMOVE_PACKET_HEADER(packet)
 VERIFY_IP(segment)
 
 // Transport Layer - Remove segment header
 appData = REMOVE_SEGMENT_HEADER(segment)
 VERIFY_PORT(appData)
 
 // Application Layer - Process data
 PROCESS(appData)
 RETURN appData
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows decapsulation where each layer removes its corresponding header as data moves up from Physical to Application layer
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is data size

examples.title: Layer Communication Flow
examples.description: Demonstrates how adjacent layers communicate in the stack
examples.code:
// Layer N+1 sends SDU (Service Data Unit) to Layer N
PROCEDURE Service_Request(layerN, serviceData)
BEGIN
 // Layer N+1 passes data to Layer N
 pdu = ADD_HEADER(layerN, serviceData)
 LAYER_N.process(pdu)
END

// Layer N provides service to Layer N+1
PROCEDURE Service_Indication(layerN, pdu)
BEGIN
 // Layer N passes data to Layer N+1
 serviceData = REMOVE_HEADER(layerN, pdu)
 LAYER_N+1.process(serviceData)
END

// Communication between peer layers (same level)
PROCEDURE Peer_Communication(senderLayer, receiverLayer, pdu)
BEGIN
 // Both layers use same protocol
 IF senderLayer.protocol == receiverLayer.protocol THEN
 TRANSMIT(pdu)
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the Service Access Point (SAP) concept where each layer provides services to the layer above through well-defined interfaces
